title: Someday Your Prince Will Be In Effect 2

jokes.text: Okay. Tough luck, Carlton. Beat it, see you, scram, sayonara, peace out. And so long, screwy.
jokes.setup: Girl says she can only go to party with one of them
jokes.punchline: Will immediately dismisses Carlton with an escalating series of dismissals ending with Bugs Bunny reference
jokes.explanation: Classic Will move - brash dismissal escalating to cartoon reference shows his character perfectly

jokes.text: Not so fast. I don't even know you. / Count your blessings.
jokes.setup: Girl protests she doesn't know Will
jokes.punchline: Will's cocky response suggests knowing him is a burden
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character-driven - Will's arrogance as self-aware joke

jokes.text: I mean while Carlton snaps at every opportunity to take a cheap shot... I'm the kindest, gentlest soul you'll ever want to meet.
jokes.setup: Will criticizes Carlton for taking cheap shots
jokes.punchline: Immediately after taking multiple cheap shots at Carlton himself
jokes.explanation: Good ironic setup showing Will's lack of self-awareness

jokes.text: You can level with me. Uncle Phil's adopted, right?
jokes.setup: Will bonding with Carlton's grandmother
jokes.punchline: Uses intimate moment to get dirt on Uncle Phil being different from family
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Will's street smarts applied to family dynamics

jokes.text: Did you know he won 'The Young Farmers Of America Pig Raising' trophy?
jokes.setup: Granny is proud of Uncle Phil
jokes.punchline: The contrast between sophisticated Uncle Phil and pig raising
jokes.explanation: Great character revelation that contrasts with Uncle Phil's current refined image

jokes.text: No. I didn't know that, but I'd love to have some details about that.
jokes.setup: Grandmother mentions Uncle Phil won pig-raising trophy
jokes.punchline: Will's fake enthusiasm to get embarrassing details
jokes.explanation: Will's mischievous nature perfectly displayed

jokes.text: Won it four years running. They had to retire his slop bucket.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil as successful pig farmer
jokes.punchline: Image of dignified lawyer's retired slop bucket as trophy
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual absurdity - fancy lawyer's retired slop bucket trophy

jokes.text: Slop bucket? I can't wait till he wakes up.
jokes.setup: Learning about Uncle Phil's pig farming past
jokes.punchline: Will's glee at having ammunition to embarrass Phil
jokes.explanation: Perfect character beat - Will's mischievous anticipation

jokes.text: Four: Get the stick out of your butt!
jokes.setup: Carlton reading his proper Palm Springs itinerary
jokes.punchline: Will adds his own crude item to the list
jokes.explanation: Classic Will/Carlton dynamic - crude vs. proper

jokes.text: What do you say to an Oreo? / I say, 'What's up, Carlton?'
jokes.setup: Carlton offers to share Oreo cookies
jokes.punchline: Will treats 'Oreo' as greeting to Carlton (black on outside, white on inside)
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that perfectly captures the show's racial dynamics - Carlton as 'Oreo' is both clever and character-specific

jokes.text: I won't be admitted to the bar. / Neither will I. The cops took my fake ID.
jokes.setup: Carlton worrying about law bar admission
jokes.punchline: Will thinks he means drinking bar, reveals fake ID
jokes.explanation: Smart misdirection playing on dual meaning of 'bar' - shows character differences perfectly

jokes.text: Come on, man, don't give me that my-dog-is-dead face.
jokes.setup: Carlton is depressed in jail
jokes.punchline: Will's colorful description of Carlton's expression
jokes.explanation: Good character voice - Will's unique way of describing emotions

jokes.text: Baby, that's man food.
jokes.setup: Date comments on Will's hamburger looking good
jokes.punchline: Will's ridiculous justification for not sharing
jokes.explanation: Typical cheap date behavior from Will, moderately funny

jokes.text: Banks. Carlton Banks.
jokes.setup: Mugger asks who Carlton is when he tries to help
jokes.punchline: Carlton introduces himself James Bond style in completely inappropriate situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - inappropriately formal introduction during mugging shows his character beautifully

jokes.text: Carlton, what the hell was that? That never happened. / It could have. Besides, when we made this bet... there was nothing in the rules that said no lying.
jokes.setup: Will calls out Carlton's obviously fake story
jokes.punchline: Carlton admits to lying but claims it's within the rules
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment acknowledging the absurdity of the storytelling device

jokes.text: Tell Michael I'll have to work on his dance steps later.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey announces Mr. Jackson is visiting
jokes.punchline: Will assumes it's Michael Jackson and casually dismisses him
jokes.explanation: Good setup for name-drop comedy, shows Will's assumed celebrity connections

jokes.text: That's Bo Jackson.
jokes.setup: Will assumes visitor is Michael Jackson
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey reveals it's actually Bo Jackson
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection payoff, relies on celebrity cameo

jokes.text: Watch the handshake, man. There goes baseball season.
jokes.setup: Bo Jackson wants to shake hands
jokes.punchline: Will warns about injuring the multi-sport athlete's hand
jokes.explanation: Topical reference to Bo Jackson's dual-sport career

jokes.text: I think that's pronounced diddley, diddley, diddley, diddley, diddley, diddley.
jokes.setup: Bo Jackson says he doesn't know diddley about cooking
jokes.punchline: Heavy D references Bo Diddley's signature rhythm
jokes.explanation: Clever musical reference from rapper Heavy D, good wordplay

jokes.text: Follow me, your running backness.
jokes.setup: Heavy D addressing Bo Jackson
jokes.punchline: Play on 'your highness' with reference to Bo's running back position
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining formal address with sports position

jokes.text: Malcolm, Malcolm, Malcolm... I mean, look, I understand your instinct... but you shouldn't try to impress a girl with who you know. It's much better that she like you just for yourself.
jokes.setup: Friend wants to name-drop Will to impress a girl
jokes.punchline: Will gives sincere advice against name-dropping while surrounded by celebrities
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment - Will giving humble advice while clearly enjoying celebrity status

jokes.text: Man, this dude knows everybody.
jokes.setup: Will introducing celebrities to each other
jokes.punchline: Another celebrity being impressed by Will's connections
jokes.explanation: Caps off the celebrity sequence but fairly predictable

jokes.text: What time should I be there, Carlton?
jokes.setup: Will says he'll choose based on physical attraction
jokes.punchline: Girl chooses Carlton despite Will's confidence
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that deflates Will's ego effectively

jokes.text: Looks like it's been on the floor.
jokes.setup: Waitress offers free peanut butter croissant
jokes.punchline: Will insults the appearance of free food
jokes.explanation: Simple insult comedy, shows Will being ungrateful

jokes.text: Especially in a place this well lit.
jokes.setup: Waitress surprised he lost the contest
jokes.punchline: Will implies he's ugly in good lighting
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating humor with environmental callback

jokes.text: Ha ha ha ha ha... ha.
jokes.setup: Phone messages from girls Will asked to the party
jokes.punchline: Extended sarcastic laughter as rejection
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey's deadpan delivery of cruel rejection makes it funny

jokes.text: I don't know exactly, but it's fair to guess that 'stunning' was part of it.
jokes.setup: Asked what description got her mistaken for shoplifter
jokes.punchline: Hilary assumes they described her as stunning
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary vanity moment - finding compliment in police description

jokes.text: Only three pencils remain. Proceed to stationery store immediately.
jokes.setup: Philip claims he bought a practical stapler, not a silly gadget
jokes.punchline: Stapler talks like a computer giving urgent supply warnings
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment that contradicts Philip's claim about practicality

jokes.text: Scary.
jokes.setup: Hilary describes her costume as gorgeous designer dress
jokes.punchline: Ashley's dry one-word assessment of Halloween appropriateness
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - one word that deflates Hilary's vanity completely

jokes.text: Janet Girlie.
jokes.setup: Carlton asks what Will's date's name is
jokes.punchline: Obviously fake name that sounds like 'Ain't got a girlie'
jokes.explanation: Clever fake name that sounds like admission of having no date

jokes.text: I wanted to come as a ballerina... but I guess a short guy with a mustache is the next best thing.
jokes.setup: Ashley explaining her costume choice
jokes.punchline: Describes herself as short guy with mustache instead of whatever she actually dressed as
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy describing Ashley's presumably masculine costume

jokes.text: Boy, you're living out all your fantasies tonight, huh?
jokes.setup: Carlton in white face makeup and lipstick
jokes.punchline: Will suggests this reveals Carlton's true desires
jokes.explanation: Typical Will jab at Carlton's costume choice

jokes.text: Well, your super fly's open.
jokes.setup: Will dressed as Superfly, Hilary commenting on costume
jokes.punchline: Play on 'your fly's open' with his Superfly costume
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining costume name with embarrassing situation

jokes.text: I'm going as Comfortable Man. / Is that a comic-book hero? / Sort of. He has superhuman bill-paying powers... so he gets to dress however he likes.
jokes.setup: Philip in regular clothes explaining his costume
jokes.punchline: Elaborate justification for not dressing up based on adult responsibilities
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dad joke - turning laziness into superhero origin story about adult privileges

jokes.text: Sort of. He has superhuman bill-paying powers... so he gets to dress however he likes.
jokes.setup: Asked if Comfortable Man is a comic book hero
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil's adult perspective on what real superpowers would be
jokes.explanation: Great adult humor that redefines superpowers in terms of real-world responsibilities

jokes.text: Did she hit her head and her eyesight come back?
jokes.setup: Carlton's date missing from party
jokes.punchline: Will suggests she regained sight and left Carlton
jokes.explanation: Clever insult suggesting only blindness could explain dating Carlton

jokes.text: Give a guy your last peanut-butter croissant... he forgets you in a minute.
jokes.setup: Will doesn't recognize Cindy the waitress
jokes.punchline: References the earlier croissant scene
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier restaurant scene

jokes.text: Baby, that hairnet was doing nothing for you.
jokes.setup: Recognizing Cindy now that she's dressed up
jokes.punchline: Blunt assessment of her work appearance
jokes.explanation: Typical Will honesty about appearance

jokes.text: Baby, that hairnet was doing nothing for you.
jokes.setup: Cindy reveals she was the waitress
jokes.punchline: Will's honest assessment of her work appearance
jokes.explanation: Typical Will honesty about appearance, character-appropriate comment

jokes.text: I guess I'm a sucker for a sad puppy.
jokes.setup: Explaining why she came to be Will's date
jokes.punchline: Compares Will to a sad puppy
jokes.explanation: Cute callback to Will's dejected state

jokes.text: This girl's a kleptomaniac. / My silverware. / That's my watch. / My date.
jokes.setup: Security guard arrests real shoplifter at party
jokes.punchline: Guests discover missing items, Carlton realizes his date was the thief
jokes.explanation: Good escalating reveals culminating in Carlton's humiliation

jokes.text: Her slipper. / Could it be?
jokes.setup: Cindy leaves behind her shoe
jokes.punchline: Will thinks he's in actual Cinderella story
jokes.explanation: Good fairy tale reference moment

jokes.text: I've been touched by magic. / Oh, my God. I can't believe I dropped my shoe. Maybe I should try some of those insole thingies. Listen, here's my number.
jokes.setup: Will having romantic Cinderella moment
jokes.punchline: Cindy returns with practical concerns about foot comfort
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of romantic moment with mundane reality

jokes.text: If you found out the only person in the world that would go out with you... was mentally deranged, you'd go to bed early, too.
jokes.setup: Ashley asking why Carlton went to bed early
jokes.punchline: Will's assessment of Carlton's date being a kleptomaniac
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Carlton's humiliation with psychological explanation

jokes.text: We were watching Matlock.
jokes.setup: Will asking why trick-or-treaters came so late
jokes.punchline: Kids prioritizing TV show over Halloween candy
jokes.explanation: Funny generational reference - kids choosing old person TV

jokes.text: But he doesn't use an axe or a chain saw... he talks his victims to death.
jokes.setup: Will describing the Bel-Air Beast monster
jokes.punchline: Monster kills by talking too much instead of weapons
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of horror tropes with social commentary

jokes.text: This monster's called the Bel-Air Beast... and, oh, my goodness, there he is!
jokes.setup: Will telling scary story about Bel-Air Beast
jokes.punchline: Points at Carlton as the monster appears
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff revealing Carlton as the talkative monster from the story

tmdb_overview: Carlton and Will, babe magnets. Who will be first to get a date for Hilary's Halloween party?
